chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS Constitutional Petition No.D-367 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on M.A No.1244/2026. 2. For orders on office objection. 3. For orders on M.A No.1245/2026. 4. For orders on M.A No.1246/2026. 5. For hearing of main case. 14.04.2026 Barrister Unaib Kamal, advocate for the petitioner a/w petitioner. = 1 Urgency granted. 2 Deferred. 3 Exemption is granted subject to all just exceptions. 4&5. Through the petition, the petitioner has challenged the disciplinary proceedings initiated by the respondents, particularly the show‐cause notice at page 19 of the file. Learned counsel submits that the petitioner is aggrieved by the alleged procedural irregularities by the respondents in failing to adhere to the Sindh Civil Servants (Efficiency and Discipline) Rules, 1973. It is further submitted that the petitioner has already filed an appeal before respondent No.3 against the impugned disciplinary action; however, the said appeal has not yet been decided. Learned A.A.G., who is present in Court in connection with other matters, waives notice of this petition and submits that any appeal filed by the petitioner shall be dealt with strictly in accordance with the law. Learned counsel for the petitioner, having been satisfied with the above statement, seeks disposal of the petition. Accordingly, the instant petition stands disposed of, with a direction to respondent No. 3 to decide the petitioner’s appeal available at page 85 of the memo of petition after providing the petitioner with a fair and meaningful opportunity of hearing, within thirty days from today, strictly in accordance with law.

Learned counsel for the petitioner, having been satisfied with the above statement, seeks disposal of the petition. Accordingly, the instant petition stands disposed of, with a direction to respondent No. 3 to decide the petitioner’s appeal available at page 85 of the memo of petition after providing the petitioner with a fair and meaningful opportunity of hearing, within thirty days from today, strictly in accordance with law. It is further directed that, until the appeal is decided, no coercive action shall be taken against the petitioner. Office is directed to communicate this order to respondent No.3 for compliance and information. JUDGE JUDGE *Saleem* 2
